body: Hey Diana, wanted to give you a heads up — I found an apartment in Millbrook, about 8 miles from our current place, still inside the same school district. Lease would start August 1. Zoe's school, her pediatrician, everything stays the same. I think it makes sense and I wanted to let you know before I signed anything.
You can't just move without telling me in advance. The parenting plan requires 30 days written notice for any change of residence. This is the first I'm hearing about it.

I am telling you in advance — August 1 is 4 weeks away. The parenting plan says 30 days notice and I'm giving that. Same school district, same distance from you, nothing changes for Zoe's schedule. I'm not asking permission, I'm keeping you informed like I'm supposed to.

I spoke with my attorney. She says the notice has to be written and delivered formally, not a text message. And she raised questions about whether this affects the custody exchange logistics. You should have discussed this with me before you even started looking. That's what co-parenting means.

I'll send an email right now if that's what you need for the record. But Diana, I'm moving 8 miles away. The exchanges don't change. Zoe's life doesn't change. I don't need your sign-off on where I choose to live.

I've thought about this more and I don't think this is in Zoe's best interest. You're moving farther from her school, which adds to her commute on your weeks. My attorney agrees that this warrants a review. I'm going to need you to hold off on signing anything.

The school is 3 miles from the new place, 4 miles from my current place. You're describing a one-mile difference. I'm not holding off — I have every legal right to move within the same county. Please have your attorney explain that to you.

Marcus, I filed an emergency motion this morning to temporarily enjoin any change of residence pending a court review. My attorney filed it today. You will be served. I'm not doing this to be difficult — I'm doing it because Zoe deserves stability and you're not thinking about that. The court will decide. This is done.
